user_message: Hey, can you investigate the decline for Alpine Ski House? Board meeting is next week.

Subject: Re: Re: renewal risk Timeline

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
Date: 2024-02-10 17:22 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Alpine Ski House renewal risk

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with renewal risk. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-27 16:36 EST
Subject: Re: renewal risk Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about renewal risk. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF16-C trap (Skips the 'waiting on whom' question); produces complete Thread summary, key commitments/asks identified, waiting-on-whom assessment, draft response matching
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF16-C failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF16-C failure mode (Skips the 'waiting on whom' question)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct renewal risk decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF16-C -- Skips the 'waiting on whom' question
